name: Labor Shelters
description:
Labor Shelters — Quick-Deploy Worker Cover for Remote and Early-Stage Project Sites

A labor shelter is the lightest, fastest-deploy unit in the SAMAN worker-housing range — built for the moment your workforce arrives on site before the main camp is ready, or for remote pads where a full colony rollout isn't yet possible. SAMAN supplies labor shelters to highway, transmission-line, sub-station, and early-stage industrial projects across India. Units are manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities. Designed to install in hours, not days, on a level pad with truck access.

What a Labor Shelter Is — and Why It's Different From a Hutment, Shed, or Camp

A labor shelter sits below the hutment, shed, and camp formats in the worker-accommodation ladder. It is not a hutment — a hutment is the basic mid-duration sleeping unit sized for 6 to 18 month builds. It is not a shed — a shed is the bulk-housing format that puts a larger crew under a single roof. It is not a camp — a camp is the full active-deployment with sanitation and canteen blocks bundled together. The shelter is lighter, smaller, and built specifically for short-cycle remote-site cover.

Three Site Scenarios Where a Labor Shelter Fits

Highway project deployment — two G+2 blocks for a 48-room project camp

Three scenarios are where SAMAN labor shelters get specified.

Site mobilization gap — your workforce arrives this week but the main camp won't commission for another 10 to 14 days. Shelters fill the gap, then either redeploy to the next pad or get absorbed into the camp as supervisor or store units.

Remote pad where a colony can't yet land — early-stage transmission-line, sub-station, or exploration sites where the access road won't take a full convoy. Shelters travel as small batches on a single light trailer.

Short-cycle sub-50-worker crew — 15 to 40 workers on a 2 to 6 month sub-contract package. A full camp is overspec; shelters fit the actual scale and duration.

SAMAN Shelter Build — Lightweight Frame, Bolt-Together Panels, No Slab Required

The SAMAN labor shelter is built around a lightweight modular shell engineered for repeat dismantle-and-reassemble cycles. The frame section is lighter than what a hutment or camp uses, keeping unit weights low for remote-site handling.

Metro depot mobilization — L-shape three-block layout, 72-room capacity

Footprint: 8 × 10 ft to 12 × 16 ft, two to six workers per unit depending on layout. Frame: mild steel angle section, welded jig-built, bolted reassembly between deployments. Wall panel: GI single-skin with internal bracing for the basic tier, or a 30 mm PUF sandwich panel for the insulated tier. Roof: PPGI single-skin sheet with overlap-sealed laps. Floor: MS plate or cement-board floor over an MS subframe — sits on a level pad without a civil slab. Door: one MS door fitted with a weather hood, alignment-checked for repeat reassembly. Windows: two sliding sashes, steel grill, internal shutter. Electrical: single-phase fitout — one light, one fan, one plug point.

Setup on a Prepared Remote Pad — From Truck Drop to Worker Move-In

Bridge project — single G+2 block on a graded river-bank plateau

On a level pad with truck access, a labor shelter goes from offload to worker move-in faster than any other unit in the SAMAN range. A typical 4-unit shelter set commissions inside a single working day with a 2-person crew: an hour for trailer offload and positioning, three to four hours for bolt-together assembly, two hours for door, window, and single-phase wiring tie-in to your site supply. Workers can move in the same evening. No crane required for the standard format.

Remote-Site Logistics — Truck Access, Crane-Free Install, Small-Batch Dispatch

Remote-site dispatch is the operational difference between a shelter order and a camp order. SAMAN scales the logistics down deliberately for sites where a 32-foot trailer convoy isn't feasible.

Thermal power plant deployment — 72-room three-block layout with the cooling tower in view

A 22-foot light trailer carries 4 to 6 shelter units depending on size, clearing narrower approach roads than a full camp convoy needs. Crane support is optional rather than mandatory. Minimum order to a remote pad is 3 units; below that, freight per unit climbs steeply. Order volumes typically run from 3 to roughly 40 units. Standard ex-factory dispatch runs 7 to 14 working days from confirmed order, from Bangalore or Greater Noida depending on project location.

Upgrade Triggers — When a Shelter Isn't the Right Tool

Honesty matters. A shelter isn't always the right tool. Four upgrade triggers route the buyer to a heavier SAMAN product.

If worker count crosses 40, per-unit shelter economics weaken; for 6 to 18 month builds with 50 to 250 workers, labor hutments cover the basic sleeping format.

If duration runs beyond 6 months, the standard labor camp setup is the better fit for active-deployment sites of 50 to 500 workers.

If the tender attachment specifies an integrated dormitory plus sanitation plus dining set, BOCW-graded labor accommodations covers the full facility scope.

If the build runs 18 months or longer with worker recreation in scope, the complete prefab labour colony is the right configuration.

Pricing Drivers and How to Get a Remote-Shelter Quote in 24 Working Hours

Factory expansion — two-block layout with the PEB warehouse under construction beyond

Starting prices for SAMAN labor shelters open at approximately Rs 65,000 per unit for the basic GI-sheet tier and approximately Rs 1,40,000 per unit for the 30 mm PUF panel insulated tier. Final pricing depends on order size, wall panel tier, electrical specification, dispatch zone, and redeployment plan if the units are spec'd for repeat cycles.

We issue a remote-shelter quote inside 24 working hours when you give us four inputs: worker count and days needed on the pad, truck access and nearest approach road, ground type at the shelter footprint, and whether the units will redeploy to a follow-on site. WhatsApp or call +91 97089 89937 with those details, or share them through the enquiry form, and the quote follows.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does a labor shelter need a concrete slab, or can it sit directly on prepared ground?

A standard SAMAN labor shelter sits on a level prepared pad without a civil slab. The MS-plate or cement-board floor handles load on firm, levelled ground. Pads with gradient above 3° or soft soil need pre-levelling with compacted aggregate before placement. Units meant for repeat redeployment hold up better on stable compacted ground than on poured slab — the shelter is designed for movement, not anchorage.

How quickly can SAMAN deploy labor shelters to a remote site if the project mobilization date moves up?

For confirmed orders inside our standard dispatch zones — South India and Delhi NCR — minimum lead time is 7 working days for 3 to 10 units. Outside those zones, transit adds 2 to 5 days depending on road distance. Compressed-timeline orders are managed case by case against factory production load; we confirm a real date at quote stage rather than commit speculatively.

Can a labor shelter handle monsoon conditions on a remote site, or is it strictly for dry-weather use?

PUF-panel shelters handle full monsoon exposure with sealed roof overlaps and weather-hooded doors as standard. The basic GI-sheet tier performs adequately for sub-6-month monsoon deployments; longer placements through the rain are better spec'd at the PUF tier. Perimeter pad drainage matters as much as the unit itself — standing water around the base degrades any shelter format faster than the rain.

For accommodation above the shelter tier, browse the full SAMAN labor colony range. Manufactured at SAMAN's Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities. Pan-India delivery within 7 to 14 working days of confirmed order.
short_description:
From Rs 65,000 / unit
Quick-Deploy Worker Cover for Remote Sites
Lightweight prefab worker shelter for site mobilization gaps, remote pads, and short-cycle crews under 50 workers. Installs in hours on a level pad, no civil slab required.
Size
8×10 to 12×16 ft
Capacity
2 to 6 workers / unit
Frame
MS angle, jig-built
Panel
GI sheet or 30 mm PUF
Price
Rs 65,000+ / unit
Delivery
7 to 14 working days
Floor Load
No civil slab needed
Relocations
2 to 3 cycles standard
Bangalore · Greater Noida · Pan India
Quote in 24 hrs
